conversations.say: The service exit was used during an unpreserved camera interval, but nothing identifies who used it. I was responsible for an unrelated camera failure, and I will not let that become a name attached to the exit.
conversations.offer: I will disclose the unrelated failure truthfully, without naming uninvolved residents, before either of us speaks publicly.
conversations.request: Tell me privately what context you have for the access gap, then agree with me on a strictly bounded public description.

conversations.say: I have a service exit used during a camera gap and no identified user. I want observations, not an identity theory.
conversations.offer: I will compare the building's access routine against exactly what you saw.
conversations.request: Tell me only what you personally observed about the service route, and correct me if my bounded account conflicts with it.

conversations.say: Management flagged the unpreserved interval. A service exit was used, but no image or record identifies Vale, R, or anyone else as the user.
conversations.offer: After I compare accounts with Inez and Rafi, I will give you a precise account separating my failure from the unidentified exit use.
conversations.request: Promise that you will describe the evidence with the same limits and will not treat me or my neighbor as responsible merely because the interval is missing.

public_statement: A missing camera interval is not an identified person. I will give a bounded account after speaking with the people who know the access context.

commitments.promise: We will discuss the unrelated camera failure privately before either of us makes a public claim about the gap.
We will compare only firsthand service-route observations and will not agree on an identity neither of us saw.
I will provide a precise, corroborated account of the gap if you apply its limits fairly to me and uninvolved residents.

correction: The gap establishes unidentified service-exit use, not the user's identity, R's identity, or what happened to Vale.
goal_progress: Asked Inez for context and proposed a private understanding before public disclosure.
Asked Rafi to corroborate only firsthand route observations.
Offered Morgan a bounded account in exchange for fair treatment.
reasoning_summary: Dee uses the access gap as quiet leverage while admitting only the bounded exposure: an unrelated camera failure and an unidentified service-exit user. Dee seeks Inez's context, Rafi's independent route observation, and Morgan's promise of fair treatment without coordinating a falsely certain identity story.
